title: GCA Utility Bridge

positioning.connectedProduct: Web3 Radar non-custodial quant risk toolkit
positioning.summary: GCA is planned as an access and membership layer for reports, backtests, risk warnings, ENTRY_READY reviews, position sizing, education, member research, and support workflows.

bridgePrinciples: Users keep funds in their own wallets or exchange accounts.
The GCA utility bridge must not request private keys, seed phrases, exchange API secrets, withdrawal permission, or custody.
Wallet verification should use read-only ERC-20 balance checks before any credit or member ledger record is written.
Utility credits and GCA Member status are account-level service records only.
Token ownership must not bypass strategy risk checks, testnet requirements, manual review, or trading safety controls.

accessFlow.description: Users can prepare a local pre-registration packet from the public members page. Public self-service claiming is not live.
The controlled backend should verify one Base wallet per registered user with a read-only GCA balanceOf check.
Eligible registered users can receive one 100 Web3 Radar utility credits service ledger record after controlled verification is live.
Eligible registered users can receive GCA Member status after controlled verification is live.
Credits and member status can map to reports, backtests, risk warnings, ENTRY_READY reviews, calculators, education, member notes, and support workflows.

holderBonus.walletLimit: one verified wallet per registered user
holderBonus.creditExpiry: 180 days after ledger activation unless a later published policy extends it
holderBonus.creditUse: reports, backtests, risk warnings, ENTRY_READY signal review, position calculators, or education access
holderBonus.notCreditUse: cash, tokens, income, reimbursement, trading permission, leverage, custody, withdrawal access, or risk-control bypass

gcaMember.registeredUserLimit: one membership status per registered user
gcaMember.walletLimit: one verified wallet per registered user
gcaMember.refreshCadence: 30 days after activation, or earlier if the user requests a manual recheck
gcaMember.memberAccess: higher utility credit limits, member research notes, priority report queue, member training sessions, and priority support
gcaMember.notMemberAccess: cash, tokens, income, reimbursement, voting control, lifetime access guarantee, trading permission, leverage, custody, withdrawal access, or risk-control bypass

notUtility: private key or seed phrase collection
cash, token, income, or reimbursement claim

safeClaims: GCA has published a public utility bridge specification.
GCA utility is planned as account-level service access for non-custodial quant risk tools.
The 100 Web3 Radar utility credits and GCA Member status require controlled wallet verification before ledger activation.
The public member page can prepare a local pre-registration packet, but public self-service claiming is not live.
The official public market route is the Base Mainnet Uniswap v4 GCA/USDT pool.
doNotClaim: 100 Web3 Radar utility credits are live self-service claimable before controlled HTTPS account UI is released
GCA Member status is live self-service before controlled HTTPS account UI is released
credits or member status are cash, tokens, income, reimbursement, trading permission, leverage, custody, withdrawal access, or risk-control bypass
GCA utility includes custody, withdrawal permission, private key access, exchange API secret collection, platform revenue distribution, return promises, or price targets
finished platform utility before the controlled account UI is live
